message:
fix(connectors): correct ended_at timestamp tracking to always use latest timestamp

All five session connectors (clawdbot, copilot, openclaw, pi_agent,
vibe) had a subtle bug in ended_at tracking: `ended_at = created.or(ended_at)`
would replace the current ended_at with an earlier timestamp whenever a
message had a timestamp, because .or() returns the first Some value
regardless of chronological ordering.

Replaced with an explicit match that compares timestamps via .max() to
ensure ended_at always advances forward to the latest seen timestamp:

  ended_at = match (ended_at, created) {
      (Some(curr), Some(ts)) => Some(curr.max(ts)),
      (None, Some(ts)) => Some(ts),
      (other, None) => other,
  };

This prevents session duration from being underreported when messages
arrive out of chronological order (common in multi-turn conversations
where system messages precede user messages in the JSON but carry later
timestamps).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
